data.title: Gold's Race to $5,000 Driven by 'Unavoidable Uncertainty,' Says RBC Capital
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) is less than 1% from reaching $5,000 per troy ounce and "unavoidable uncertainty" could drive the precious metal even higher, according to RBC Capital Global Head of Commodity Strategy Christopher Looney.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"If we are to take any lesson from 2025, it's that the number of peaks can still increase, especially when investors are looking for a hedge against uncertainties they struggle to price elsewhere," Looney said in a note to clients.

Gold Rises as Uncertainty Drives Safe‑Haven Flows

Gold has continued to lead as a top safe-haven asset, driven by recent geopolitical events including the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro, protests in Iran, and President Trump's threat to tariff eight European countries over control of Greenland. Trump later pulled a U-turn on the European tariffs, although the unpredictability of his policies continues to benefit the precious metal.

RBC expects gold to remain in a range between $4,500 and $5,000 with an average price of $4,600 this year. At the same time, Looney noted that gold could surge even higher in the event of a "real risk-off scenario."

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Notches Up With New Order Revelation
data.description:
A key point recently emerged for aerospace stock Boeing (BA), where it was found that, for the first time since 2018, Boeing landed more orders than Airbus (EADSY), its immediate rival. With orders on the rise, and several major airlines throwing in with Boeing, the idea that Boeing has surpassed its limitations and made its comeback is not out of line. Boeing investors were willing to believe this idea, and gave Boeing shares a fractional boost in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Reports note that the combination of growing overall orders and growing support from the biggest airlines is coming together to give investors new confidence. Moreover, major industry conversations are starting to gather around Boeing to give it even more potential advantage.

Airlines are looking at Boeing and talking about fleet expansions, fleet renewals, and production capacity issues. This is good news for Boeing; after years of concern about quality, concerns about production, and the like, airlines are talking about Boeing like any other valuable, trusted, regular supplier. And with Boeing on track to get its 737 MAX 7 and 737 MAX 10 jets certified this year, that is one more point in Boeing's favor when it comes to figuring out where to pick up the next round of jets.

Unexpectedly Supersonic Cargo Jet

Cargo jets do not usually go supersonic. There is not much point in aircraft designed to ferry goods around breaking the sound barrier. But a Boeing 747 cargo jet managed to do just that, even if inadvertently. The story itself may be a bit old, but it helps remind us what Boeing can do, and what it may be able to do down the line.

Way back in December 1991, a Boeing 747 cargo jet developed a failure warning on its inertial navigation systems. The result: the plane went 90 degrees to the right, and went into a steep decline, heading downward between 30 and 35 degrees. The plane lost 10,000 feet of altitude before finally stabilizing at around 22,500 feet. At one point, the plane reached Mach 1.25, which is actually supersonic speed. Other reports suggested the plane never cleared Mach 0.98. Given the plane's maximum speed was rated at Mach 0.92, however, that still represents a real feat.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, two Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 42.8% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $258.85 per share implies 2.44% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Meta and EssilorLuxottica Hit With Patent Lawsuit over Smart Glasses
data.description:
Tech giant Meta Platforms (META) is facing a lawsuit over its smart glasses. More specifically, Solos Technology, a smaller smart-eyewear company, filed a patent infringement case in federal court in Massachusetts against Meta, along with eyewear giant EssilorLuxottica (ESLOY) and its Oakley brand. Solos claims that the companies knowingly used its patented smart-glasses technology and is seeking billions of dollars in damages. It is also asking for an injunction that could potentially disrupt sales of Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In its complaint, Solos says that Oakley and EssilorLuxottica were exposed to its technology years ago through meetings and product testing. It also alleges that research written in 2021 by MIT Sloan Fellow Priyanka Shekar cited Solos' patents and that she later brought that knowledge to Meta as a product manager. Interestingly, though, the two companies have had very different market outcomes.

In fact, Solos began working on smart glasses about a decade ago, first targeting cyclists and later adding AI features like translation and ChatGPT integration. However, its consumer products have struggled, with weak Amazon (AMZN) reviews and complaints about customer support. In contrast, Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses have received strong feedback, and Meta and EssilorLuxottica have even discussed doubling production to keep up with demand.

What Is the Price Target for Meta?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on META stock based on 37 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average META price target of $820.21 per share implies 23.2% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: BlackRock's Rick Rieder Emerges as a Top Fed Chair Candidate
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) Chief Investment Officer of Global Fixed Income Rick Rieder is now considered a frontrunner to be selected by President Trump to lead the Federal Reserve.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On prediction platform Polymarket, Rieder's odds have more than quadrupled to 35% from 8% over the past week. Former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh leads all candidates with 42% odds, while current Governor Christopher Waller trails in third place at 12%.

Trump Says BlackRock's Rieder is 'Very Impressive'

Earlier this week, Trump said he had narrowed down his shortlist to two or three candidates. "And I probably can tell you, we're down to maybe one, in my mind," he told CNBC. Trump also said Rieder was "very impressive" after the pair sat down for a meeting.

Meanwhile, the odds of National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett being selected have fallen to 6% from 15% over the past week after Trump emphasized that he would like him to remain in his current role.

data.title: Meta Platforms (META) Blocks Teen Access to AI Characters
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) has temporarily blocked teenagers from accessing artificial intelligence-generated (AI) characters on its social media apps.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company stressed that the block is temporary as it develops an updated version of AI characters for teens to access and use on social media sites such as Facebook and Instagram. The temporary block comes days before a case against Meta Platforms that is set to go to trial in New Mexico.

In the legal case, Meta is accused of not making an effort to protect children and teenagers from sexual exploitation on its family of apps. Media reports recently surfaced claiming that Meta has sought to limit discovery related to social media's impact on teen mental health, accusations the company denies.

Social Media Risks and Impacts

In recent months, Meta Platforms announced parental controls for AI characters that appear on its social media platforms, allowing parents to monitor topics and block access to certain characters. Meta said parents would be able to completely turn off chats with AI characters, if they choose to do so.

The parental controls were set to be released this year, but the company is now turning off AI characters altogether for teens while it updates the AI characters to a newer version. In a written statement, Meta said that it has heard the concerns of parents that they wanted more insights and control over their teens' interactions with AI characters.

There are growing concerns about the impacts of social media and AI on youth mental health.

Is META Stock a Buy?

The stock of Meta Platforms has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 44 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 37 Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average META price target of $820.21 implies 26.65% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Slides Despite Rising Consumer Sentiment; Tech Stock Recovery
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to end the trading week with a loss, shrugging off a rise in consumer sentiment.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

January's Consumer Sentiment Index showed a reading of 56.4, higher than the consensus estimate of 54.0 and rising from 52.9 in December. A reading above 60 signals confidence, while a reading below 60 signals pessimism. Sentiment improved broadly across several demographic groups, including income, education, political affiliation, and age. However, the index is still down by 21% from 71.7 a year ago.

Furthermore, consumers expect year-ahead inflation of 4.0%, the lowest since January 2025 and down from 4.2% in the prior month. Their long-run inflation expectations inched higher to 3.3% from 3.2%. "In comparison, readings ranged between 2.8% and 3.2% in 2024, and were below 2.8% throughout 2019 and 2020," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu in a reference to long-run expectations.

Meanwhile, precious metals may be attracting capital away from equities. Both gold (XAUUSD) and silver (XAGUSD) secured new record highs on Friday, with the former racing towards $5,000 per troy ounce and the latter pushing past $100 for the first time.

Renewed geopolitical uncertainty stemming from President Trump's European tariff threats has contributed to the upside in precious metals. While Trump stepped back from the tariffs, investors have continued to bid up gold and silver after he announced the U.S. would soon impose 25% secondary tariffs on countries that transact business with Iran.

The Dow Jones is down by 0.49% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Economists Expect Bank of Canada to Hold Interest Rates Steady in 2026
data.description:
A majority of economists expect that the Bank of Canada will keep its trendsetting overnight interest rate on hold in 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Economists polled by Reuters (TRI) expect Canada's central bank to remain on the sidelines this year as the domestic economy strengthens and inflation remains subdued. The Bank of Canada last decided to hold its key interest rate at 2.25% in December and signaled that an extended pause is likely.

A total of 35 economists surveyed by Reuters said that the central bank is likely to leave interest rates unchanged at its next policy meeting on Jan. 28. Nearly 75% of economists, 26 of 35, forecast that the Bank of Canada will leave rates unchanged for the entire year.

U.S.-Canada Trade

The Bank of Canada lowered interest rates a total of 2.75% between June 2024 ‌and October 2025, one of the most aggressive cuts among G10 central banks. The rate cuts came as the neighboring U.S. levied steep tariffs on Canadian goods and services.

However, Canada's economy has stayed resilient despite U.S. tariffs of 25% to 50% on key sectors such as automobiles, lumber, and steel. The risk of renewed trade tensions with the U.S., Canada's top trade partner, is the one wild card facing the domestic economy, say economists. The U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement on Trade (USMCA) comes up for a scheduled review this July.

Is SHOP Stock a Buy?

Let's look at the stock of Shopify (SHOP), a leading Canadian company. It has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 31 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 18 Buy and 13 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average SHOP price target of $180.64 implies 30.94% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: PepsiCo Goes 'All In' on Zero Sugar as Diet Sodas Become 'Radioactive'
data.description:
Beverage and snack company PepsiCo (PEP) has changed how it markets its sodas by putting Pepsi Zero Sugar front and center. In fact, the company brought back the Pepsi Challenge last year for the first time in 50 years, but instead of using regular Pepsi or Diet Pepsi, it compared Pepsi Zero Sugar against Coca-Cola Zero Sugar. And, as Bloomberg reports, that same focus will continue at the upcoming Super Bowl, where Pepsi's expensive TV ad will feature Zero Sugar rather than the original soda. According to Pepsi, the company has gone "all in" on Zero Sugar, with most of its marketing budget now spent promoting it.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, this shift is part of a larger trend across the soda industry. While Diet and Zero Sugar drinks are both calorie-free and use artificial sweeteners, Zero Sugar branding connects better with younger consumers. This is because many Gen Z and wellness-minded shoppers avoid sugar but also dislike diet culture and calorie counting, which makes the word "diet" feel outdated and "radioactive," according to Malachite Strategy and Research CEO Kevin Ryan. As a result, Zero Sugar drinks drove 52% of U.S. soda sales growth last year, even as sales of full-sugar sodas and diet options fell.

In addition, both Pepsi and Coca-Cola (KO) sold more Zero Sugar products in 2025, despite an overall decline in soda consumption. Other soda makers, including Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP), are making similar moves by replacing diet sodas with Zero Sugar versions and adding more flavors. Therefore, with younger consumers frequently trying new drinks, soda companies see Zero Sugar as a key way to stay relevant and drive growth.

Is PEP Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on PEP stock based on seven Buys, eight Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average PEP price target of $161.85 per share implies 12.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Toyota Motor (TM) Recalls 162,000 Tundra Vehicles in U.S.
data.description:
Japanese automaker Toyota Motor Corp. (TM) has recalled 162,000 of its Tundra pickup trucks in the United States due to multimedia display issues.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a news release, Toyota said that the recall impacts 2024-2025 Toyota Tundra and Tundra Hybrid models. According to the automaker, the problem involves the multimedia display in the trucks, which can become stuck on a camera view or display a black image while the vehicle is engaged.

Toyota did not provide details about the potential safety implications of the display malfunction or the timeline for repairs in its announcement. However, the company said that impacted Tundra owners will be notified and that the display issue will be fixed at licensed Toyota dealers.

Toyota's Strategy

The Tundra recall is the largest one in the U.S. in years and comes as the automaker executes an aggressive electrification strategy. Toyota has a goal of achieving 1.5 million electric vehicle sales a year by 2026, and selling 3.5 million battery-powered vehicles annually by 2030.

The company is also developing a method for mass producing solid-state batteries for electric vehicles and aims to commercialize the technology. Toyota has prioritized electric vehicle production even though rivals such as General Motors (GM) and Ford Motor Co. (F) have scaled back their EV ambitions.

Is TM Stock a Buy?

Not enough Wall Street analysts cover Toyota Motor Corp. So instead, we'll look at the three-month performance of TM stock. As one can see in the chart below, Toyota's share price has risen 10.55% in the last 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: "Stronger Growth": Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Surges Up With AI Developments
data.description:
Today was actually pretty big for tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) and its work in artificial intelligence development. Not only did we get a look at what analysts think of the Fairwater data centers, we also got a look at a whole new application. This combination of factors brought great cheer from investors, who sent Microsoft shares launching up over 4% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

First, word from UBS analyst Karl Keirstead, who has a five-star rating on TipRanks, maintained his Buy rating on Microsoft, though pared back the price target from $650 per share to $600. Keirstead found that there were not likely to be significant delays in getting the Fairwater facilities up and running out in Wisconsin. With no real delay to speak of, the Fairwater facilities were likely to add value to Azure, and fuel further growth for Microsoft.

And what is Microsoft doing with some of this AI capacity? One report noted Microsoft was giving Paint users the ability to make their own coloring book pages. Only Windows Insiders will have access to this tool, at least for now. But with it, users can offer text-based prompts of what they want to see on the page—"a cute fluffy cat on a donut" was one example—and the image will render, but without color, for users to add later.

Fable Bringing Xbox Games Studios Back?

Meanwhile, the return of the Developer Direct show brought with it one major new development that caught a lot of attention. Specifically, the return of Fable caught a lot of attention at the show. Early reports suggest that the new Fable draws on some of the best parts of the old Fable, like "…reactivity, comical combat, and unapologetically British comedy."

Further, the new Fable will also boast a "…huge, seamless open world…," which should be welcome to those who prefer their adventures deep and immersive. However, the new game will not be paying much attention to the timelines of previous games, instead going off on its own within the Albion setting.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 1.59% rally in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $626.14 per share implies 33.01% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
